stories.title: Real Madrid legend Ancelotti rivals Barcelona great Guardiola for Italy job
stories.summary: Former Man City and Barca boss Pep Guardiola is not the clear candidate for Italian job, as Carlo Ancelotti approached. The Italian Football Federation (FIGC) has talked to former Manchester City coach Pep Guardiola and Brazil boss Carlo Ancelotti about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup.

stories.title: Cristian Volpato: Australian World Cup star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
stories.summary: Australia’s World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tested positive for cocaine after he was stopped for driving over the speed limit in Sydney, police said. The 22-year-old made three appearances for Australia at the World Cup and was one of the team’s breakout stars, having switched allegiances from Italy before the tournament. New South Wales police said Volpato was allegedly clocked driving at 109km/h, going 50km/h over the speed limit, when he was stopped on Sydney’s Anzac Bridge at around 1am on Friday morning. Volpato was then subjected to a roadside drug test which returned a positive result for cocaine, New South Wales police said in a statement.

stories.title: Elon Musk warns AI may overtake human intelligence in five years, renews call for safety collaboration
stories.summary: Elon Musk believes artificial intelligence could exceed the combined intelligence of humans within the next five years, while warning that people may no longer remain in control of the technology over the coming decade. Speaking in an interview with The Economist, the Tesla and xAI chief said AI is evolving at a pace that could eventually outperform humans across nearly every intellectual task. He said the only area where humans may retain a unique advantage is "being human. " Despite continuing to acknowledge risks associated with advanced AI, Musk said he now sees rapid progress in the field as inevitable.

stories.title: Government review finds 'no adverse findings' linking Aughinish Alumina to Russian military
stories.summary: The Department of Enterprise confirmed the review into Aughinish Alumina has established "no adverse findings" linking alumina produced at the Limerick plant to Russia's weapons industry. However, it found a "lack of verifiable internal trade data" since the country's invasion of Ukraine. The review follows reports that alumina exports from the plant on the Shannon Estuary, Co Limerick, are being used to supply Russian weapons manufacturers.

stories.title: Charli xcx: Music, Fashion, Film review – slippery search into selfhood that hits just as hard as Brat
stories.summary: A fter a decade spent trailblazing a unique kind of pop freedom from the genre’s fringes, A-list success seemed like the thing that might finally trap Charli xcx . Her 2024 club-rat opus Brat spawned memes, embarrassing political bandwagoning, Collins’ word of the year and possibly terminal damage to the bra industry. Its ludicrous cultural impact looked set to pickle her as a caricature of a Parliament-toting bad girl; the question of whether “Brat summer” could last – whether Charli could sustain the interest – was so rife she turned it into a mockumentary, The Moment .

stories.title: 'My last match was World Cup final': Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi announces international retireme
stories.summary: Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi has announced his retirement from international football, ending a remarkable 17-year career with the national team. The 38-year-old leaves as one of Argentina's most successful defenders, having won the 2022 FIFA World Cup and two Copa America titles. His final appearance came in Argentina's 1-0 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup final in the United States. Otamendi made 139 appearances for Argentina and was a key figure in the team's success over the years. After the final, he shared an emotional message on Instagram, thanking the country for giving him the chance to achieve his biggest dream.

stories.title: Oprah Winfrey sparks fresh backlash after she revisits Prince Harry and Meghan Markle bombshell interview
stories.summary: Oprah Winfrey is facing a new wave of backlash over an old royal bombshell. After revisiting her 2021 interview with Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, Winfrey's explanation of her "silent or silenced" question has sparked renewed debate online. The media mogul's reflection on one of the most talked-about moments from the explosive sit-down has reignited debate over the interview that shook the monarchy, divided viewers and put Harry and Meghan's claims about royal life under a global microscope.

stories.title: Darren Purse takes over at Sligo Rovers
stories.summary: Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 09:22 Sligo Rovers have appointed Darren Purse as manager Former Premier League defender Darren Purse has been appointed as the new Sligo Rovers manager on an 18-month contract. Purse, who played for two seasons with Birmingham City in the English top flight, arrives at the Showgrounds from Cardiff City, where he spent almost five years working with the club's Under-23 and U21 sides, running their loan programme and serving a spell as assistant manager of the first team. He replaces John Russell, who ended an eight-year spell with the Connacht club to take over at Shelbourne earlier this month.

stories.title: Arsenal set to make £70m bid for Newcastle's Guimaraes
stories.summary: Arsenal are set to make a £70m offer for Newcastle captain Bruno Guimaraes. Brazil international Guimaraes is a priority target for the Gunners, with the midfielder keen to join the Premier League champions. Newcastle are aware of the 28-year-old's preference to join Arsenal this summer. Talks over a potential move for Guimaraes are ongoing and are believed to have accelerated in recent days. The agreement of personal terms is not expected to be an issue, though it remains to be seen if Arsenal 's £70m offer will meet Newcastle 's valuation.

stories.title: ‘Ted Lasso’ Was Finished. Then Jason Sudeikis Had an Epiphany
stories.summary: In early February 2024, Jason Sudeikis convened the writers of Ted Lasso in Ojai, California. At his urging, the show had aired its third and purportedly final season a year or so earlier, but now he was having second thoughts. “I was like, ‘Oh, I don’t know if it’s done,’ ” says the creator and star. So, he gathered everyone at the Ojai Valley Inn to see whether there was more story to mine. By then, a few different ideas for potential spinoffs had been bandied about. Brett Goldstein and co-creator Joe Kelly had started working on an idea about former captain Roy Kent, while another two staffers played around with one about the character Keeley Jones.

stories.title: Code in iOS 27 lets Apple deactivate leased iPhones, report says
stories.summary: The iPhone 18 is about to launch, along with the final version of its operating system, iOS 27, most likely in September. But a new report says it may arrive with a feature that lets Apple, and third-party lenders, remotely deactivate phones if customers fall behind on payments. With the iOS 27 beta now widely available, 9to5Mac found code that would let Apple place delinquent iPhones in "Restricted Mode. " This would limit users to a short list of widgets and apps, including the App Store, Clock, Settings, Wallet, and Passwords, according to the report.

stories.title: Sami Sheen says dad Charlie ‘hasn’t given me a dime’ — touts OnlyFans to support herself
stories.summary: Charlie Sheen’s daughter, Sami Sheen, shut down rumors that her famous father funds her luxurious lifestyle, while crediting OnlyFans for her success. “Look, if I had Daddy’s money, I would own it, OK? I would let you guys know,” the model shared via a TikTok video Saturday in response to a comment that the actor gives her money. “These comments make me so frustrated because this man hasn’t given me a dime in over four years. I have been fully financially supporting myself since I was 18 years old,” she said. Charlie Sheen’s daughter, Sami Sheen (seen above with her dad, mom Denise Richards and sister Lola), addressed rumors that the actor pays for her lifestyle.

stories.title: Love Island fans find 'proof' of Priya's game-playing after clash with Julia
stories.summary: LOVE Island fans are convinced Priya has accidentally proved Julia’s claim that she really is a “game player”. The pair clashed repeatedly in Thursday’s episode after Julia insisted Priya and Aidan had coupled up because they were days away from the final and felt it would be “safer” to stick with OGs. Love Island fans claim Priya has proved she really is a game player Credit: ITV Julia accused her of coupling up with Aidan because he’s an OG Credit: ITV Julia shared her opinion during the explosive Grafties awards and the row carried over into the dressing room.

stories.title: HMRC issues two-week warning for 864,000 taxpayers
stories.summary: HMRC has issued a two-week warning for 864,000 taxpayers after a new tax system was introduced. Making Tax Digital is a new way to report income tax digitally. It became mandatory from April 2026 for sole traders and landlords with qualifying income over £50,000. As part of the new process, taxpayers have to submit quarterly updates for their income and expenses - and the deadline for the first update is August 7. Your quarterly update has to be sent through HMRC -recognised software. After each update, you will see an estimate of their tax bill - you still then need to send a tax return as normal on January 31. The deadline to pay any tax owed is also still January 31.

stories.title: Cristian Volpato: Australia midfielder allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding in Sydney
stories.summary: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato has allegedly tested positive for cocaine after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on the same night. The 22-year-old Australia midfielder was stopped in a BMW Coupe on the Anzac Bridge at 11:05pm local time on Thursday night after he was caught travelling between 86-94km/h in a 60km/h zone, police said. He was subjected to a roadside breath test, which returned a negative result. However, Volpato allegedly returned a positive result for cocaine when he was stopped two hours later for travelling at 109km/h in the same 60km/h zone.
sources: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation

stories.title: Kerry election candidate jailed for contempt after ‘manifestly unfair’ hearing, High Court rules
stories.summary: A judge in Kerry conducted a “manifestly unfair” hearing in which he sent independent election candidate Michelle Keane to prison for contempt of court over an alleged refusal to take down a social media post about a garda, the High Court said. Keane is to seek an award of damages over her jailing. Judge Brian Cregan made the comments as he overturned a finding of contempt by Judge James O’Donohoe in Listowel Circuit Court on December 10th, 2024, when he jailed Keane after she allegedly refused to give an undertaking not to remove the post.

stories.title: Gary Lineker’s tax hypocrisy
stories.summary: Credit: Getty The UK’s king of virtue signalling has served up another blinder. Gary Lineker has joined 119 fellow millionaires in signing an open letter calling on Andy Burnham to raise their taxes. The smug-fest, organised by the tediously grandstanding campaign group Patriotic Millionaires, declared: ‘We can afford it. We’re not talking about higher taxes on those who get up and go to work for their income every day, but on the very richest whose income is derived from the wealth they hold. ’ Curiously, while promoting his valiant plea to surrender more of his fortune, dear Gary neglected to mention that as recently as March 2023 he took HMRC to a tribunal over a disputed £4.

stories.title: War in Ukraine | Global Conflict Tracker
stories.summary: Updated July 23, 2026 Four years since Russia’s February 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Russia still occupies roughly 20 percent of the country after gaining almost five thousand square kilometers of territory in 2025. Russia continues to bombard Ukrainian cities, while Ukraine maintains drone attacks on Russian oil infrastructure and military sites. Since January 2022, Ukraine has received about $188 billion in aid from the United States and $197 billion from the European Union. Fighting and air strikes have inflicted nearly 56,000 civilian casualties [PDF] , while 3. 7 million people are internally displaced , and 5. 9 million are registered as refugees . 10.

stories.title: Newcastle analysis: Incoming Bamba has high potential and shows transfer strategy shift
stories.summary: Newcastle United's move for Aladji Bamba underlines a marked shift in transfer strategy for the Magpies. It is certainly hard to imagine the club spending £55m on a 29-year-old again like they did when they signed forward Yoane Wissa from Brentford last summer. Instead, Newcastle have targeted players aged 20 and under on the continent this time around. Bamba, like Sean Steur, Ewen Jaouen and Bazoumana Toure, will need time to adapt to the physicality and intensity of the Premier League - and it is worth stressing the 20-year-old Frenchman has made only 12 competitive starts for Monaco.

stories.title: ICC member states decide fate of embattled prosecutor Karim Khan
stories.summary: In May 2025, Khan had stepped back from his duties to fight the allegations made by one of his female colleagues, after an inquiry was launched the previous year. The 56-year-old Briton was then suspended last month by a key governing body of the ICC, the 21-member bureau of the Assembly of State Parties (ASP), which called Friday's meeting at UN headquarters in New York. At that time, the bureau said the suspension was "not an indication of the final outcome" in the matter. The decision on whether to relieve Khan of his duties is now subject to a secret ballot by the 125 countries party to the International Criminal Court during a closed-door session. An absolute majority carries the vote.

stories.title: Zapatero Insists on Innocence Amid Scrutiny Over Jewelry Origins
stories.summary: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero has publicly defended himself after questions emerged regarding the origin of jewels valued at €1. 3 million found in his possession. In a recent televised interview, Zapatero stated that he is absolutely convinced of his innocence, emphasizing that the jewels were a long-standing courtesy gift with no intention of personal enrichment. This statement has reignited debate over the standards of political responsibility and transparency in Spain. Zapatero's approach to the controversy stands out for its reliance on personal conviction rather than detailed explanations.

stories.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
stories.summary: The divorce, involving the chairman of one of South Korea's biggest companies, has gripped the nation. The chairman of corporate giant SK Group has been ordered by a South Korean court to pay his ex-wife 944bn won (£483m; $644m) in a case that local media has called the " divorce of the century ". The award, which still needs to be finalised, is below the initial 1. 38tn won that chairman Chey Tae-won was told to pay Roh Soh-yeong in 2024. It comes more than a decade after his marriage to Roh - the daughter of a former president - fell apart following the revelation he had fathered a child with another woman.

stories.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
stories.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners in the latest escalation in the trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year. Those targeted, including the UK, China and the European Union, will face a tariff of 10% to 12. 5% on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. The tariffs, which replace an identical levy which expires on Friday, are based on claims key economic partners have failed to properly tackle forced labour.

stories.title: Why have thousands taken to the streets? India's 'cockroach' protest explained
stories.summary: India is seeing one of its biggest protests in recent years. But why did it start? And why is the mascot a cockroach? Here's what you need to know. In India, tens of thousands of Gen Z protesters have taken to the streets of the capital Delhi and other cities, calling for the resignation of the country's education minister. Starting as a satirical online movement just a couple of months ago, the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) is now one of India's most visible youth movements. But what drove the movement? The BBC's Anahita Sachdev explains.

stories.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
stories.summary: The housing secretary tells the BBC that the Renters Rights' Act is already having a significant impact on the market. Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has said Prime Minister Andy Burnham is not looking at bringing in rent controls in England. She told BBC Breakfast that changes made through the Renters' Rights Act, which came into effect in May, were "already having a significant impact on the market". She added that controls which had been implemented in some areas of the UK over the past few years had not "necessarily brought rents down". In 2023 when he was the mayor of Greater Manchester, Burnham called for freezes to private sector rents until the cost of living crisis was over.

stories.title: Man Utd bring in Leicester goalkeeper Leitzig
stories.summary: Manchester United sign goalkeeper Janina Leitzig from Leicester City for an undisclosed fee. Manchester United have signed goalkeeper Janina Leitzig from Leicester City for an undisclosed fee. The 27-year-old becomes their second signing of the summer following the arrival of defender Andrea Medina. It is believed the former Germany youth international has signed a three-year deal at United after they triggered the release clause in her Leicester contract, which was renewed in 2025. Leitzig has been at Leicester for three years, initially on a season-long loan from Bayern Munich, where she became a regular starter.

stories.title: Scotland's Gunn concedes four in San Jose debut defeat
stories.summary: Scotland goalkeeper Angus Gunn suffers a nightmare debut for San Jose Earthquakes as they slump to a 4-0 defeat at home to Orlando City in their first match following Major League Soccer's break for the World Cup. Visiting centre-half David Brekalo was left unmarked as he headed home Eduard Atuesta's free-kick with just seven minutes gone. Winger Ivan Angulo doubled City's lead in the 29th minute with a powerful low drive into the far corner from just outside the penalty box.

stories.title: Preview: Preston North End vs Cambridge United - prediction, team news, lineups
stories.summary: Preston North End and Cambridge United will continue their preparations for the 2026-27 season on Friday, when they meet for a friendly in Spain. The two sides both headed out for pre-season camps ahead of their upcoming terms in the Championship and League One respectively and will play their third friendlies. Match preview Preston North End will return to action in a friendly on Friday as part of their pre-season trip to Spain as they gear up for a 12th consecutive season in the Championship. Their latest term ended in a 14th-placed finish under Paul Heckingbottom , dropping into the bottom half as they lost three of their last four games to end on 60 points from 46 matches.

stories.title: Truck Festival opens to record number of revellers
stories.summary: Image caption, Truck Festival began on Thursday By Martin Eastaugh at Truck Festival and Ethan Gudge South of England Oxfordshire's largest music festival is set to celebrate its biggest ever year, with record crowds arriving for the first day of Truck Festival. Truck 2026 is taking place at Hill Farm in Steventon, Oxfordshire, from 23 to 26 July, with indie bands The Maccabees, Two Door Cinema Club, The Wombats all headlining over the weekend. Ahead of the festival's wider start on Friday, some people with early access tickets arrived on Thursday - where they were entertained by Irish artist CMAT on the main stage.

stories.title: Once the region’s safest bet, Jordan’s tourism industry now hangs in the balance
stories.summary: Once the region’s safest bet, Jordan’s tourism industry now hangs in the balance FCDO advises against travel to Aqaba airport and seaport after recent Iran attacks on the Kingdom, but key tourist sites remain unaffected Greg Dickinson Senior Travel Writer Show biography Greg Dickinson is an award-winning travel writer and co-host of The Travel Expert podcast. Before joining The Telegraph, he edited and researched guidebooks for Rough Guides, DK Eyewitness and Bradt Travel Guides.

stories.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
stories.summary: Regional mayors are to be given a leading role in the UK’s economic policy, Andy Burnham has said, as he called the opening of No 10 North “the biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen”. The new prime minister has revived the national economic council [NEC], first set up by Gordon Brown to put the government on a war footing during the 2008 financial crash, arguing that economic growth requires the same level of urgency, focus and collective action. In an article for the Guardian , he wrote: “When I entered Downing Street, I told staff that the era of fighting devolution is over. No one in government loses when power is shared.

stories.title: ‘It was the moment I lost innocence’: how a childhood stop-and-search led to the British debut movie of the year
stories.summary: I mran Perretta always knew that the title of his debut feature film would be “Ish” – short for Ishmail, the name of the 12-year-old boy from Luton at the heart of his story. Only later did people point out the similarity to other coming-of-age films with three letters in the title: Kes by Ken Loach, and Satyajit Ray’s Apu trilogy. Now, as the reviews trickle in, the comparisons to Kes and Apu keep coming. Like them, Ish is a poetic film that grabs you by the heart and pulls you into a child’s perspective on the world. The response has been lovely, says Perretta, and a relief. “It’s nice that people have compared Ish to those films rather than Kidulthood. I did worry about that.

stories.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
stories.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula. French authorities have ordered the evacuation of the entirety of the Cap Ferret peninsula (Lège-Cap Ferret) on the south-west coast, with hundreds of people escaping the area by boat. The tourism destination is being evacuated as firefighters continue to battle a wildfire that has raged in the Gironde region for days. The fire, which began on Tuesday in Saumos, continued to burn overnight with 8,700 hectares (around 21,500 acres) consumed by the flames.

stories.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
stories.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street. Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester later on Friday. The branch of Downing Street in the North West will form part of the prime minister's aim to shift power from Whitehall and Westminster to regions across the country. Ministers Lucy Powell and Louise Haigh will also operate regularly from the new site, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join them by the end of next year. On Friday in Manchester, Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee first launched in 2008 to respond to economic challenges around the UK.

stories.title: Who am I? Guess The Hundred cricket star No 4
stories.summary: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible? Welcome to a special cricket edition of our Who am I? game. The rules are simple. For each day of The Hundred there's a cricketer, taking part in this year's tournament, who we want you to identify in as few attempts as possible. After each wrong guess you unlock a new clue. The sooner you guess the answer, the more points you score. Three is a good score, four or five points is exceptional. Take part and return for more tomorrow. Today's player and clues set by BBC Sport's Joe Rindl. After more quizzes? Go to our dedicated Sports Quizzes page and sign up for notifications to get the latest quizzes sent straight to your device.

stories.title: Unlike Joe Duffy, Kieran Cuddihy doesn’t ramp up the drama on Liveline
stories.summary: For any broadcaster, the ability to see both sides of the story is a crucial skill, but it’s mandatory on a show such as Liveline ( RTÉ Radio 1 , weekdays), where everyone basically thinks they’re in the right. Even so, Kieran Cuddihy goes above and beyond in his duty as host of the phone-in show, going one step farther by contradicting his own argument even as he makes it. On Tuesday, as callers debate whether helmets and high-vis gear should be compulsory for cyclists , Cuddihy suggests that legislating on such matters is meaningless without proper enforcement. As an example, he points to the legal requirement for lights on bikes after dark. “It’s not enforced.

stories.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
stories.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

stories.title: Four in a row: Sounders hit new low
stories.summary: The Seattle Sounders followed up their worst performance of the MLS era with another absolute stinker, losing 3–1 on the road against an Austin FC side mired near the bottom of the Western Conference table. It’s the first time since 2013 that the Sounders have lost as many as four straight regular-season games and they’ve now been outscored 11–2 in that span. Ilie Sanchez opened the scoring in the 11th minute before Paul Rothrock equalized in the 26th minute.

stories.title: Faced With Heat and Wildfires, France Turns to Spain to Learn How to Cope
stories.summary: A French government delegation will travel to its southern neighbor to learn how it copes with long spells of hot weather.

stories.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
stories.summary: The meeting comes hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike. Representatives of India's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will hold talks with the government on Friday as thousands of people continue to protest day and night demanding the education minister's resignation. The meeting follows an eventful night where activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his hunger strike after 26 days of fasting in solidarity with the protests. Prime Minister Narendra Modi also posted a video promising a tough new law on examination paper leaks, the issue at the core of the protests.

stories.title: Rosalía Thrills Costa Rican Fans in Bogotá with Song Dedication and National Flag
stories.summary: “Concert stage atmosphere with vibrant stage lighting, artist holding a national flag on stage in front of a cheering crowd, realistic live performance concert photography style. ” During her massive concert in Bogotá, Spanish singer Rosalía delivered one of the most emotional moments of the night by sending a warm shout-out to a group of Costa Rican fans who traveled all the way to Colombia just to see her perform live. The gesture, which quickly went viral across social media, highlighted the artist’s close connection with her Latin American fanbase and the dedication of concertgoers who cross borders for her live shows. 1.

stories.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
stories.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

stories.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
stories.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

stories.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
stories.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

stories.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
stories.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

stories.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
stories.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, is one of 48 female veterans to benefit from the groups' help. Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, has been working with the group and found it beneficial to her mental health. She experienced PTSD following multiple deployments to war zones around the world, where she helped injured soldiers who were being rescued from battles and taken to hospital. Tulloch said she was really desperate at times. "I just went through a lot of low moods, nightmares, seeing things, hearing things, smelling things, doubting myself, not being able to do things that I could do. "It really does affect the way your brain thinks.

stories.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
stories.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

stories.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
stories.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States

stories.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
stories.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

stories.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
stories.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

stories.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
stories.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.

offbeat.title: Real Madrid legend Ancelotti rivals Barcelona great Guardiola for Italy job
offbeat.summary: Former Man City and Barca boss Pep Guardiola is not the clear candidate for Italian job, as Carlo Ancelotti approached. The Italian Football Federation (FIGC) has talked to former Manchester City coach Pep Guardiola and Brazil boss Carlo Ancelotti about taking over the crisis-hit national team. It hopes to announce the new Italy coach within the week, with the Azzurri still reeling after failing for a third straight time to qualify for the World Cup.

offbeat.title: Cristian Volpato: Australian World Cup star allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding
offbeat.summary: Australia’s World Cup star Cristian Volpato allegedly tested positive for cocaine after he was stopped for driving over the speed limit in Sydney, police said. The 22-year-old made three appearances for Australia at the World Cup and was one of the team’s breakout stars, having switched allegiances from Italy before the tournament. New South Wales police said Volpato was allegedly clocked driving at 109km/h, going 50km/h over the speed limit, when he was stopped on Sydney’s Anzac Bridge at around 1am on Friday morning. Volpato was then subjected to a roadside drug test which returned a positive result for cocaine, New South Wales police said in a statement.

offbeat.title: Elon Musk warns AI may overtake human intelligence in five years, renews call for safety collaboration
offbeat.summary: Elon Musk believes artificial intelligence could exceed the combined intelligence of humans within the next five years, while warning that people may no longer remain in control of the technology over the coming decade. Speaking in an interview with The Economist, the Tesla and xAI chief said AI is evolving at a pace that could eventually outperform humans across nearly every intellectual task. He said the only area where humans may retain a unique advantage is "being human. " Despite continuing to acknowledge risks associated with advanced AI, Musk said he now sees rapid progress in the field as inevitable.

offbeat.title: Government review finds 'no adverse findings' linking Aughinish Alumina to Russian military
offbeat.summary: The Department of Enterprise confirmed the review into Aughinish Alumina has established "no adverse findings" linking alumina produced at the Limerick plant to Russia's weapons industry. However, it found a "lack of verifiable internal trade data" since the country's invasion of Ukraine. The review follows reports that alumina exports from the plant on the Shannon Estuary, Co Limerick, are being used to supply Russian weapons manufacturers.

offbeat.title: Charli xcx: Music, Fashion, Film review – slippery search into selfhood that hits just as hard as Brat
offbeat.summary: A fter a decade spent trailblazing a unique kind of pop freedom from the genre’s fringes, A-list success seemed like the thing that might finally trap Charli xcx . Her 2024 club-rat opus Brat spawned memes, embarrassing political bandwagoning, Collins’ word of the year and possibly terminal damage to the bra industry. Its ludicrous cultural impact looked set to pickle her as a caricature of a Parliament-toting bad girl; the question of whether “Brat summer” could last – whether Charli could sustain the interest – was so rife she turned it into a mockumentary, The Moment .

offbeat.title: 'My last match was World Cup final': Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi announces international retireme
offbeat.summary: Argentina defender Nicolas Otamendi has announced his retirement from international football, ending a remarkable 17-year career with the national team. The 38-year-old leaves as one of Argentina's most successful defenders, having won the 2022 FIFA World Cup and two Copa America titles. His final appearance came in Argentina's 1-0 defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup final in the United States. Otamendi made 139 appearances for Argentina and was a key figure in the team's success over the years. After the final, he shared an emotional message on Instagram, thanking the country for giving him the chance to achieve his biggest dream.

offbeat.title: Oprah Winfrey sparks fresh backlash after she revisits Prince Harry and Meghan Markle bombshell interview
offbeat.summary: Oprah Winfrey is facing a new wave of backlash over an old royal bombshell. After revisiting her 2021 interview with Prince Harry and Meghan Markle, Winfrey's explanation of her "silent or silenced" question has sparked renewed debate online. The media mogul's reflection on one of the most talked-about moments from the explosive sit-down has reignited debate over the interview that shook the monarchy, divided viewers and put Harry and Meghan's claims about royal life under a global microscope.

offbeat.title: Darren Purse takes over at Sligo Rovers
offbeat.summary: Updated / Friday, 24 Jul 2026 09:22 Sligo Rovers have appointed Darren Purse as manager Former Premier League defender Darren Purse has been appointed as the new Sligo Rovers manager on an 18-month contract. Purse, who played for two seasons with Birmingham City in the English top flight, arrives at the Showgrounds from Cardiff City, where he spent almost five years working with the club's Under-23 and U21 sides, running their loan programme and serving a spell as assistant manager of the first team. He replaces John Russell, who ended an eight-year spell with the Connacht club to take over at Shelbourne earlier this month.

offbeat.title: Arsenal set to make £70m bid for Newcastle's Guimaraes
offbeat.summary: Arsenal are set to make a £70m offer for Newcastle captain Bruno Guimaraes. Brazil international Guimaraes is a priority target for the Gunners, with the midfielder keen to join the Premier League champions. Newcastle are aware of the 28-year-old's preference to join Arsenal this summer. Talks over a potential move for Guimaraes are ongoing and are believed to have accelerated in recent days. The agreement of personal terms is not expected to be an issue, though it remains to be seen if Arsenal 's £70m offer will meet Newcastle 's valuation.

offbeat.title: ‘Ted Lasso’ Was Finished. Then Jason Sudeikis Had an Epiphany
offbeat.summary: In early February 2024, Jason Sudeikis convened the writers of Ted Lasso in Ojai, California. At his urging, the show had aired its third and purportedly final season a year or so earlier, but now he was having second thoughts. “I was like, ‘Oh, I don’t know if it’s done,’ ” says the creator and star. So, he gathered everyone at the Ojai Valley Inn to see whether there was more story to mine. By then, a few different ideas for potential spinoffs had been bandied about. Brett Goldstein and co-creator Joe Kelly had started working on an idea about former captain Roy Kent, while another two staffers played around with one about the character Keeley Jones.

offbeat.title: Code in iOS 27 lets Apple deactivate leased iPhones, report says
offbeat.summary: The iPhone 18 is about to launch, along with the final version of its operating system, iOS 27, most likely in September. But a new report says it may arrive with a feature that lets Apple, and third-party lenders, remotely deactivate phones if customers fall behind on payments. With the iOS 27 beta now widely available, 9to5Mac found code that would let Apple place delinquent iPhones in "Restricted Mode. " This would limit users to a short list of widgets and apps, including the App Store, Clock, Settings, Wallet, and Passwords, according to the report.

offbeat.title: Sami Sheen says dad Charlie ‘hasn’t given me a dime’ — touts OnlyFans to support herself
offbeat.summary: Charlie Sheen’s daughter, Sami Sheen, shut down rumors that her famous father funds her luxurious lifestyle, while crediting OnlyFans for her success. “Look, if I had Daddy’s money, I would own it, OK? I would let you guys know,” the model shared via a TikTok video Saturday in response to a comment that the actor gives her money. “These comments make me so frustrated because this man hasn’t given me a dime in over four years. I have been fully financially supporting myself since I was 18 years old,” she said. Charlie Sheen’s daughter, Sami Sheen (seen above with her dad, mom Denise Richards and sister Lola), addressed rumors that the actor pays for her lifestyle.

offbeat.title: Love Island fans find 'proof' of Priya's game-playing after clash with Julia
offbeat.summary: LOVE Island fans are convinced Priya has accidentally proved Julia’s claim that she really is a “game player”. The pair clashed repeatedly in Thursday’s episode after Julia insisted Priya and Aidan had coupled up because they were days away from the final and felt it would be “safer” to stick with OGs. Love Island fans claim Priya has proved she really is a game player Credit: ITV Julia accused her of coupling up with Aidan because he’s an OG Credit: ITV Julia shared her opinion during the explosive Grafties awards and the row carried over into the dressing room.

offbeat.title: HMRC issues two-week warning for 864,000 taxpayers
offbeat.summary: HMRC has issued a two-week warning for 864,000 taxpayers after a new tax system was introduced. Making Tax Digital is a new way to report income tax digitally. It became mandatory from April 2026 for sole traders and landlords with qualifying income over £50,000. As part of the new process, taxpayers have to submit quarterly updates for their income and expenses - and the deadline for the first update is August 7. Your quarterly update has to be sent through HMRC -recognised software. After each update, you will see an estimate of their tax bill - you still then need to send a tax return as normal on January 31. The deadline to pay any tax owed is also still January 31.

offbeat.title: Cristian Volpato: Australia midfielder allegedly tests positive for cocaine after speeding in Sydney
offbeat.summary: Socceroos World Cup star Cristian Volpato has allegedly tested positive for cocaine after police twice stopped him for speeding in Sydney on the same night. The 22-year-old Australia midfielder was stopped in a BMW Coupe on the Anzac Bridge at 11:05pm local time on Thursday night after he was caught travelling between 86-94km/h in a 60km/h zone, police said. He was subjected to a roadside breath test, which returned a negative result. However, Volpato allegedly returned a positive result for cocaine when he was stopped two hours later for travelling at 109km/h in the same 60km/h zone.
sources: ABC News & Headlines – Australian Broadcasting Corporation

offbeat.title: Kerry election candidate jailed for contempt after ‘manifestly unfair’ hearing, High Court rules
offbeat.summary: A judge in Kerry conducted a “manifestly unfair” hearing in which he sent independent election candidate Michelle Keane to prison for contempt of court over an alleged refusal to take down a social media post about a garda, the High Court said. Keane is to seek an award of damages over her jailing. Judge Brian Cregan made the comments as he overturned a finding of contempt by Judge James O’Donohoe in Listowel Circuit Court on December 10th, 2024, when he jailed Keane after she allegedly refused to give an undertaking not to remove the post.

offbeat.title: Gary Lineker’s tax hypocrisy
offbeat.summary: Credit: Getty The UK’s king of virtue signalling has served up another blinder. Gary Lineker has joined 119 fellow millionaires in signing an open letter calling on Andy Burnham to raise their taxes. The smug-fest, organised by the tediously grandstanding campaign group Patriotic Millionaires, declared: ‘We can afford it. We’re not talking about higher taxes on those who get up and go to work for their income every day, but on the very richest whose income is derived from the wealth they hold. ’ Curiously, while promoting his valiant plea to surrender more of his fortune, dear Gary neglected to mention that as recently as March 2023 he took HMRC to a tribunal over a disputed £4.

offbeat.title: War in Ukraine | Global Conflict Tracker
offbeat.summary: Updated July 23, 2026 Four years since Russia’s February 2022 full-scale invasion of Ukraine, Russia still occupies roughly 20 percent of the country after gaining almost five thousand square kilometers of territory in 2025. Russia continues to bombard Ukrainian cities, while Ukraine maintains drone attacks on Russian oil infrastructure and military sites. Since January 2022, Ukraine has received about $188 billion in aid from the United States and $197 billion from the European Union. Fighting and air strikes have inflicted nearly 56,000 civilian casualties [PDF] , while 3. 7 million people are internally displaced , and 5. 9 million are registered as refugees . 10.

offbeat.title: Newcastle analysis: Incoming Bamba has high potential and shows transfer strategy shift
offbeat.summary: Newcastle United's move for Aladji Bamba underlines a marked shift in transfer strategy for the Magpies. It is certainly hard to imagine the club spending £55m on a 29-year-old again like they did when they signed forward Yoane Wissa from Brentford last summer. Instead, Newcastle have targeted players aged 20 and under on the continent this time around. Bamba, like Sean Steur, Ewen Jaouen and Bazoumana Toure, will need time to adapt to the physicality and intensity of the Premier League - and it is worth stressing the 20-year-old Frenchman has made only 12 competitive starts for Monaco.

offbeat.title: ICC member states decide fate of embattled prosecutor Karim Khan
offbeat.summary: In May 2025, Khan had stepped back from his duties to fight the allegations made by one of his female colleagues, after an inquiry was launched the previous year. The 56-year-old Briton was then suspended last month by a key governing body of the ICC, the 21-member bureau of the Assembly of State Parties (ASP), which called Friday's meeting at UN headquarters in New York. At that time, the bureau said the suspension was "not an indication of the final outcome" in the matter. The decision on whether to relieve Khan of his duties is now subject to a secret ballot by the 125 countries party to the International Criminal Court during a closed-door session. An absolute majority carries the vote.

offbeat.title: Zapatero Insists on Innocence Amid Scrutiny Over Jewelry Origins
offbeat.summary: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero has publicly defended himself after questions emerged regarding the origin of jewels valued at €1. 3 million found in his possession. In a recent televised interview, Zapatero stated that he is absolutely convinced of his innocence, emphasizing that the jewels were a long-standing courtesy gift with no intention of personal enrichment. This statement has reignited debate over the standards of political responsibility and transparency in Spain. Zapatero's approach to the controversy stands out for its reliance on personal conviction rather than detailed explanations.

offbeat.title: Why have thousands taken to the streets? India's 'cockroach' protest explained
offbeat.summary: India is seeing one of its biggest protests in recent years. But why did it start? And why is the mascot a cockroach? Here's what you need to know. In India, tens of thousands of Gen Z protesters have taken to the streets of the capital Delhi and other cities, calling for the resignation of the country's education minister. Starting as a satirical online movement just a couple of months ago, the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) is now one of India's most visible youth movements. But what drove the movement? The BBC's Anahita Sachdev explains.

offbeat.title: Preview: Preston North End vs Cambridge United - prediction, team news, lineups
offbeat.summary: Preston North End and Cambridge United will continue their preparations for the 2026-27 season on Friday, when they meet for a friendly in Spain. The two sides both headed out for pre-season camps ahead of their upcoming terms in the Championship and League One respectively and will play their third friendlies. Match preview Preston North End will return to action in a friendly on Friday as part of their pre-season trip to Spain as they gear up for a 12th consecutive season in the Championship. Their latest term ended in a 14th-placed finish under Paul Heckingbottom , dropping into the bottom half as they lost three of their last four games to end on 60 points from 46 matches.

offbeat.title: Truck Festival opens to record number of revellers
offbeat.summary: Image caption, Truck Festival began on Thursday By Martin Eastaugh at Truck Festival and Ethan Gudge South of England Oxfordshire's largest music festival is set to celebrate its biggest ever year, with record crowds arriving for the first day of Truck Festival. Truck 2026 is taking place at Hill Farm in Steventon, Oxfordshire, from 23 to 26 July, with indie bands The Maccabees, Two Door Cinema Club, The Wombats all headlining over the weekend. Ahead of the festival's wider start on Friday, some people with early access tickets arrived on Thursday - where they were entertained by Irish artist CMAT on the main stage.

offbeat.title: Once the region’s safest bet, Jordan’s tourism industry now hangs in the balance
offbeat.summary: Once the region’s safest bet, Jordan’s tourism industry now hangs in the balance FCDO advises against travel to Aqaba airport and seaport after recent Iran attacks on the Kingdom, but key tourist sites remain unaffected Greg Dickinson Senior Travel Writer Show biography Greg Dickinson is an award-winning travel writer and co-host of The Travel Expert podcast. Before joining The Telegraph, he edited and researched guidebooks for Rough Guides, DK Eyewitness and Bradt Travel Guides.

offbeat.title: Unlike Joe Duffy, Kieran Cuddihy doesn’t ramp up the drama on Liveline
offbeat.summary: For any broadcaster, the ability to see both sides of the story is a crucial skill, but it’s mandatory on a show such as Liveline ( RTÉ Radio 1 , weekdays), where everyone basically thinks they’re in the right. Even so, Kieran Cuddihy goes above and beyond in his duty as host of the phone-in show, going one step farther by contradicting his own argument even as he makes it. On Tuesday, as callers debate whether helmets and high-vis gear should be compulsory for cyclists , Cuddihy suggests that legislating on such matters is meaningless without proper enforcement. As an example, he points to the legal requirement for lights on bikes after dark. “It’s not enforced.

offbeat.title: Four in a row: Sounders hit new low
offbeat.summary: The Seattle Sounders followed up their worst performance of the MLS era with another absolute stinker, losing 3–1 on the road against an Austin FC side mired near the bottom of the Western Conference table. It’s the first time since 2013 that the Sounders have lost as many as four straight regular-season games and they’ve now been outscored 11–2 in that span. Ilie Sanchez opened the scoring in the 11th minute before Paul Rothrock equalized in the 26th minute.

offbeat.title: India's 'cockroach' protesters and government to hold talks as stalemate continues
offbeat.summary: The meeting comes hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk announced he was ending his 26-day hunger strike. Representatives of India's Cockroach Janta Party (CJP) will hold talks with the government on Friday as thousands of people continue to protest day and night demanding the education minister's resignation. The meeting follows an eventful night where activist Sonam Wangchuk ended his hunger strike after 26 days of fasting in solidarity with the protests. Prime Minister Narendra Modi also posted a video promising a tough new law on examination paper leaks, the issue at the core of the protests.

offbeat.title: Rosalía Thrills Costa Rican Fans in Bogotá with Song Dedication and National Flag
offbeat.summary: “Concert stage atmosphere with vibrant stage lighting, artist holding a national flag on stage in front of a cheering crowd, realistic live performance concert photography style. ” During her massive concert in Bogotá, Spanish singer Rosalía delivered one of the most emotional moments of the night by sending a warm shout-out to a group of Costa Rican fans who traveled all the way to Colombia just to see her perform live. The gesture, which quickly went viral across social media, highlighted the artist’s close connection with her Latin American fanbase and the dedication of concertgoers who cross borders for her live shows. 1.

breaking.title: Tech titan ordered to pay ex-wife $644m in divorce settlement
breaking.summary: The divorce, involving the chairman of one of South Korea's biggest companies, has gripped the nation. The chairman of corporate giant SK Group has been ordered by a South Korean court to pay his ex-wife 944bn won (£483m; $644m) in a case that local media has called the " divorce of the century ". The award, which still needs to be finalised, is below the initial 1. 38tn won that chairman Chey Tae-won was told to pay Roh Soh-yeong in 2024. It comes more than a decade after his marriage to Roh - the daughter of a former president - fell apart following the revelation he had fathered a child with another woman.

breaking.title: US hits dozens of trading partners with new wave of tariffs
breaking.summary: Imposed citing forced labour concerns, the levies replace a temporary global duty brought in after the US Supreme Court struck down tariffs in February. The US has imposed new tariffs on 60 trading partners in the latest escalation in the trade war reignited by US President Donald Trump when he returned to office last year. Those targeted, including the UK, China and the European Union, will face a tariff of 10% to 12. 5% on all goods, accounting for almost all American imports. The tariffs, which replace an identical levy which expires on Friday, are based on claims key economic partners have failed to properly tackle forced labour.

breaking.title: Faced With Heat and Wildfires, France Turns to Spain to Learn How to Cope
breaking.summary: A French government delegation will travel to its southern neighbor to learn how it copes with long spells of hot weather.

politics.title: Angela Rayner rules out rent controls in England
politics.summary: The housing secretary tells the BBC that the Renters Rights' Act is already having a significant impact on the market. Housing Secretary Angela Rayner has said Prime Minister Andy Burnham is not looking at bringing in rent controls in England. She told BBC Breakfast that changes made through the Renters' Rights Act, which came into effect in May, were "already having a significant impact on the market". She added that controls which had been implemented in some areas of the UK over the past few years had not "necessarily brought rents down". In 2023 when he was the mayor of Greater Manchester, Burnham called for freezes to private sector rents until the cost of living crisis was over.

politics.title: Regional mayors to take key roles in UK policy decisions as part of No 10 North plans
politics.summary: Regional mayors are to be given a leading role in the UK’s economic policy, Andy Burnham has said, as he called the opening of No 10 North “the biggest rebalancing of power our country has seen”. The new prime minister has revived the national economic council [NEC], first set up by Gordon Brown to put the government on a war footing during the 2008 financial crash, arguing that economic growth requires the same level of urgency, focus and collective action. In an article for the Guardian , he wrote: “When I entered Downing Street, I told staff that the era of fighting devolution is over. No one in government loses when power is shared.

politics.title: Burnham to spend first day working from northern branch of No 10
politics.summary: Various ministers are also set to work regularly from the northern branch of Downing Street. Andy Burnham will hold his first meeting at the new No 10 North in Manchester later on Friday. The branch of Downing Street in the North West will form part of the prime minister's aim to shift power from Whitehall and Westminster to regions across the country. Ministers Lucy Powell and Louise Haigh will also operate regularly from the new site, with up to 300 civil servants expected to join them by the end of next year. On Friday in Manchester, Burnham will chair a meeting of the National Economic Council, a cabinet committee first launched in 2008 to respond to economic challenges around the UK.

sports.title: Man Utd bring in Leicester goalkeeper Leitzig
sports.summary: Manchester United sign goalkeeper Janina Leitzig from Leicester City for an undisclosed fee. Manchester United have signed goalkeeper Janina Leitzig from Leicester City for an undisclosed fee. The 27-year-old becomes their second signing of the summer following the arrival of defender Andrea Medina. It is believed the former Germany youth international has signed a three-year deal at United after they triggered the release clause in her Leicester contract, which was renewed in 2025. Leitzig has been at Leicester for three years, initially on a season-long loan from Bayern Munich, where she became a regular starter.

sports.title: Scotland's Gunn concedes four in San Jose debut defeat
sports.summary: Scotland goalkeeper Angus Gunn suffers a nightmare debut for San Jose Earthquakes as they slump to a 4-0 defeat at home to Orlando City in their first match following Major League Soccer's break for the World Cup. Visiting centre-half David Brekalo was left unmarked as he headed home Eduard Atuesta's free-kick with just seven minutes gone. Winger Ivan Angulo doubled City's lead in the 29th minute with a powerful low drive into the far corner from just outside the penalty box.

sports.title: Who am I? Guess The Hundred cricket star No 4
sports.summary: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible? Welcome to a special cricket edition of our Who am I? game. The rules are simple. For each day of The Hundred there's a cricketer, taking part in this year's tournament, who we want you to identify in as few attempts as possible. After each wrong guess you unlock a new clue. The sooner you guess the answer, the more points you score. Three is a good score, four or five points is exceptional. Take part and return for more tomorrow. Today's player and clues set by BBC Sport's Joe Rindl. After more quizzes? Go to our dedicated Sports Quizzes page and sign up for notifications to get the latest quizzes sent straight to your device.

entertainment.title: ‘It was the moment I lost innocence’: how a childhood stop-and-search led to the British debut movie of the year
entertainment.summary: I mran Perretta always knew that the title of his debut feature film would be “Ish” – short for Ishmail, the name of the 12-year-old boy from Luton at the heart of his story. Only later did people point out the similarity to other coming-of-age films with three letters in the title: Kes by Ken Loach, and Satyajit Ray’s Apu trilogy. Now, as the reviews trickle in, the comparisons to Kes and Apu keep coming. Like them, Ish is a poetic film that grabs you by the heart and pulls you into a child’s perspective on the world. The response has been lovely, says Perretta, and a relief. “It’s nice that people have compared Ish to those films rather than Kidulthood. I did worry about that.

facts.title: France orders evacuation of tourist spot as hundreds flee wildfires by boat
facts.summary: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula. French authorities have ordered the evacuation of the entirety of the Cap Ferret peninsula (Lège-Cap Ferret) on the south-west coast, with hundreds of people escaping the area by boat. The tourism destination is being evacuated as firefighters continue to battle a wildfire that has raged in the Gironde region for days. The fire, which began on Tuesday in Saumos, continued to burn overnight with 8,700 hectares (around 21,500 acres) consumed by the flames.

facts.title: Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site
facts.summary: The small bird, which is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe, is seen on camera. Rare night heron spotted at National Trust site Figure caption, By Clara Bullock Somerset A bird rarely seen in the UK has been caught on camera in Somerset, raising hopes that work to restore freshwater habitats could provide a refuge for it. The black-crowned night heron is widespread across Africa, Asia, the Americas and Europe but has now been spotted at National Trust's Holnicote Estate, near Minehead. This comes as conservationists at the estate introduced new freshwater habitats, providing a refuge for the heron, but also water voles, otters, pine martens, and even white-tailed eagles.

facts.title: Play area aims to interest children in rare habitat
facts.summary: A science centre hopes play is the key to making children care about the future of chalk streams. The future of one of England's most unique habitats could rest with play areas designed for children to interact with the rare environment, a science centre hopes. More than eight in 10 of the world's chalk streams are found in England, but the current prolonged hot and dry weather puts pressure on the wildlife that calls the rare waterways home. Winchester Science Centre, in Hampshire, has now created a play zone themed on the local landscape in the hope children will connect with the environments into adulthood.

health.title: One year, 17 towns, dozens of voices: what we heard along the coast of England and Wales
health.summary: I f you were asked to summon up images of the coastline of England and Wales, you might think of sunny days, buckets, spades and carefree holidays. But for many young people growing up by the sea, decades of evidence tell a very different story. For them, rather than a picture-perfect horizon full of hope, the sea has become a boundary that swallows up opportunity. With a 73% decline in funding for youth services in England over the past 12 years, and a lack of opportunities that has led to nearly 1 million 16- to 24-year-oldsnot in work, education or training (Neet), the situation for young people, especially in places of deprivation, has never seemed more desperate.

health.title: Replacing gas stove can help asthmatics as much as some drugs, US experts find
health.summary: A US study has found a 70% drop in hospital admissions and days off from work or school when people with poorly controlled asthma had their gas stove and hob replaced with electric. Ohio’s publicly owned MetroHealth began the study in December 2024 but funding was terminated early by the incoming Trump administration. It had been designed to investigate if replacing gas cooking improved asthma for 1,200 people living near Cleveland, Ohio. Prof Ash Sehgal of Case Western Reserve University, the project’s leader, said: “We had completed the project at fewer than 100 households. As a result, over 1,100 households were unable to benefit. We also had to lay off about 20 project staff.

health.title: Female veterans support group makes national impact
health.summary: Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, is one of 48 female veterans to benefit from the groups' help. Chelley Tulloch, a former RAF medic, has been working with the group and found it beneficial to her mental health. She experienced PTSD following multiple deployments to war zones around the world, where she helped injured soldiers who were being rescued from battles and taken to hospital. Tulloch said she was really desperate at times. "I just went through a lot of low moods, nightmares, seeing things, hearing things, smelling things, doubting myself, not being able to do things that I could do. "It really does affect the way your brain thinks.

crisis.title: Brent Crude Oil Prices Top $100 as Conflict With Iran Drags On
crisis.summary: Stocks in Asia were down following a sell-off in the U.S. markets over soaring oil prices, rising borrowing costs and worries about the technology industry.

crisis.title: Trump Administration Imposed New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations
crisis.summary: The administration launched new duties tied to countries’ imports of goods made with forced labor, replacing a 10 percent tariff immediately after it expired.

crisis.title: Trump’s Global Tariffs: What’s the Latest on the Trade War
crisis.summary: The president has unveiled a complicated system to forge ahead with his trade war, after the Supreme Court stuck down his original levies.
entities: Supreme Court of the United States

tech.title: Google fined €890m by EU for favouring its own apps over rivals
tech.summary: It is the first major enforcement action against the tech giant under the EU's new digital rules. Google has been fined €890m (£759m) by the EU for allegedly abusing its power by favouring its own apps and services over those from rivals. It is the first major action against the tech giant under the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA), a landmark law aimed at curbing the power of the world's biggest tech companies. The Commission argued Google's practices limited consumer choice and gave its own services an unfair advantage over rivals. But the firm criticised the decision, arguing the EU's requirements could damage services used by millions of European customers.

tech.title: Judge Extends Pause for Paramount-Warner Bros. Deal
tech.summary: The court blocked the companies from closing the acquisition through mid-August, as a judge considers two lawsuits challenging it.

tech.title: Intel Benefits From a New Shift in A.I. Spending
tech.summary: The Silicon Valley chipmaker’s revenue rose 25 percent in the latest quarter, its fastest growth in 15 years, as A.I. firms increasingly bought chips known as central processing units.
